metadata.title: SOCRadar Entra ID Integration for Microsoft Sentinel
metadata.description: Pulls leaked employee credentials from SOCRadar (Botnet, PII Exposure, VIP Protection) and takes automated remediation actions in Microsoft Entra ID. Logs to Microsoft Sentinel custom tables.
metadata.author: SOCRadar

metadata.description: Microsoft Sentinel Log Analytics Workspace Name
Location/region of the Log Analytics workspace (e.g. northeurope). Leave empty to use the deployment resource group’s location.
Resource group of the Log Analytics workspace. Leave empty to use the current deployment resource group (default — same RG as Function App).
SOCRadar Platform API Key (used for Botnet, PII Exposure, VIP Protection sources)
SOCRadar Company ID
SOCRadar Platform Base URL

App Registration Client ID (multi-tenant App with signInAudience=AzureADMultipleOrgs). Same ID across all tenants. Auth is secretless via Managed Identity plus Federated Identity Credential on the primary tenant’s app object.

Comma-separated Microsoft Entra ID Tenant IDs to query for compromised users. The first tenant in the list is the primary one ’where the multi-tenant App Registration and the UAMI Federated Identity Credential live. Additional tenants must consent the same App Registration (signInAudience=AzureADMultipleOrgs). For single-tenant deployments, leave this empty and set EntraIdTenantId instead.
Legacy single-tenant ID. Only used when EntraIdTenantIds is empty (backward compat). New deployments should use EntraIdTenantIds.
Entra ID Security Group ID for compromised users (required if EnableAddToGroup=true)
Enable Botnet Data v2 source
Enable PII Exposure v2 source
Enable VIP Protection v2 source (UNVERIFIED: endpoint not in official API documentation)

Look up each leaked identity in Microsoft Entra ID before taking actions. Requires User.Read.All. If false, User.Read.All becomes optional and the app only fetches SOCRadar data and writes to Log Analytics.
Revoke all active sign-in sessions for matched users. Requires EnableUserLookup=true and User.RevokeSessions.All.
Add matched users to a security/quarantine group. Requires EnableUserLookup=true, GroupMember.ReadWrite.All, and SecurityGroupId.
Remove matched users from the security group. Requires EnableUserLookup=true, GroupMember.ReadWrite.All, and SecurityGroupId.
Force password change at next sign-in for matched users. Requires EnableUserLookup=true and User-PasswordProfile.ReadWrite.All.
Disable matched user accounts. Requires EnableUserLookup=true and User.EnableDisableAccount.All. High impact; use with caution.
Re-enable previously disabled accounts. Requires EnableUserLookup=true and User.EnableDisableAccount.All.

Delete all non-password authentication methods (Microsoft Authenticator, phone, FIDO2, software OATH, Windows Hello, email, TAP) to force MFA re-registration at next sign-in. Works on all Entra ID tiers. Requires EnableUserLookup=true and UserAuthenticationMethod.ReadWrite.All. HIGH IMPACT — off by default.
Confirm matched users as compromised in Identity Protection. Requires EnableUserLookup=true, IdentityRiskyUser.ReadWrite.All, and Entra ID P1/P2 licensing.

Validate leaked credentials via ROPC (Resource Owner Password Credentials). Only possible with plaintext passwords. Requires Allow public client flows on the App Registration. Microsoft discourages ROPC in production.
Create Microsoft Sentinel incidents for compromised accounts
Automatically resolve SOCRadar alarms when compromised user is found in Entra ID
Write plaintext passwords to Log Analytics tables. By default only password_present/masked fields are stored. Enable only if your security policy requires it.
How often to check for new leaked credentials (1-24 hours)
Fallback: minutes of history to fetch on first run if InitialStartDate is empty. Default 43200 = 30 days.

Optional date (YYYY-MM-DD format, e.g. 2026-04-01) for the first fetch. Takes priority over InitialLookbackMinutes. Leave empty to use the lookback window. Not a number of minutes — use date format only.
Max pages fetched per source per timer run (page size 100). Default 50 covers most customers. Raise to 100-200 for large backlog imports; keep 50 for steady-state low-volume.
Run function immediately on Function App start, in addition to the timer schedule. Default true (recommended for first deploy verification). Set false if you only want scheduled runs.

properties.description: SOCRadar Entra ID Integration — Logs Ingestion API

properties.scriptContent: sleep 30 && az functionapp restart --name $FA_NAME --resource-group $RG_NAME && echo 'Function App restarted'
